data.title: Perspire Sauna Studio partners with Dose to offer liquid supplements at all locations, enhancing post-sauna wellness.
data.description: As of January 29, 2026, Dose's scientifically endorsed liquid supplements are now being sold at all Perspire Sauna Studio outlets in an effort to enhance customer wellness. This move aims to provide support towards improving guests' health beyond just the sauna experience. Perspire Sauna Studio, recognized as the leading sauna franchise in the nation, offers customers the opportunity to access these supplements to further enhance their wellness routines.

data.title: RBFCU and True Sky Federal Credit Union approve merger agreement, combining storied credit unions.
data.description: Two well-established credit unions, Randolph-Brooks Federal Credit Union (RBFCU) and True Sky Federal Credit Union, rooted in aviation, are joining forces in Live Oak, Texas. The boards of directors at RBFCU and True Sky unanimously approved the merger on January 29, 2026. This collaboration will bring together the assets and membership bases of both credit unions to create a stronger financial institution moving forward. The merger marks a significant milestone in the aviation community, with RBFCU holding assets of $10 billion and True Sky with assets totaling $500 million.

data.title: ALGO may hit $0.14 by Feb 2026, with potential for 23% gains. Experts predict $0.16-$0.19 in medium term despite bearish signals.
data.description: The cryptocurrency Algorand (ALGO) has the potential to increase by 23%, aiming to break through the resistance at $0.14. Analysts predict a medium-term range of $0.16 to $0.19, despite current signals indicating a bearish market momentum.

data.title: Global conveyor system market to hit USD 11.9B by 2035 from USD 6.9B in 2025, showing a CAGR of 5.5%.
data.description: In Newark, Delaware, as of January 29, 2026, industry data shows that the global conveyor system market is expected to grow significantly, reaching a value of USD 11.9 billion by 2035. This marks a substantial increase from its estimated worth of USD 6.9 billion in 2025. The projected growth indicates a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.5% over the forecast period. This data underscores the market's upward trajectory and potential for expansion in the coming years.

data.title: Marriott Bonvoy hosts "Pup Rally" adoption events for Puppy Bowl XXII, bringing dogs to hotels in Big Game teams' hometowns on Feb. 8.
data.description: Marriott Bonvoy, the Official Hospitality Partner of Puppy Bowl XXII, will be hosting adoptable puppies at hotels in the hometowns of the teams competing in the Big Game on February 8. This initiative is set to take place in various cities on Feb. 8, 2026, including Bethesda, Md. The lovable pups will showcase their special talents in bringing joy through wagging tails and cheerful smiles as part of the heartwarming event. Further details and locations will be revealed closer to the date.

data.title: Mozambique LNG project fully restarts onshore and offshore operations after lifting Force Majeure declared in 2021.
data.description: In a meeting between Patrick Pouyanné, the Chairman and CEO of TotalEnergies, and President Daniel Chapo of Mozambique, it was announced today in Afungi the complete resumption of activities for the Mozambique LNG project. The decision to restart project operations onshore and offshore comes after the Mozambique LNG consortium lifted the Force Majeure that was declared in 2021 on November 7, 2025. TotalEnergies is listed on the Paris (TTE), LSE (TTE), and NYSE (TTE) stock exchanges.
Palestinian media reports at least two dead and several wounded in an Israeli drone strike in central Gaza's Maghazi camp.

data.title: OmniTaaS named 'Emerging Leader' in RFID market by MarketsandMarkets, specializing in asset tracking and supply chain solutions.
data.description: A business based in Delray Beach, Florida, OmniTaaS, has gained acknowledgement as a promising company in the RFID industry. The company focuses on RFID technology for immediate asset monitoring, extensive supply chain visibility, and manufacturing coordination. This recognition as an 'Emerging Leader' in the RFID sector was received on January 29, 2026.

data.title: Inventors from MN create Shoes Ahoy, a shoe organizer for boats, to enhance boating experience.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 29, 2026, two inventors and boat enthusiasts from Victoria, MN, introduced Shoes Ahoy, a product designed to improve the boating experience. The innovators aim to simplify shoe organization and maintain cleanliness on boats with their invention, which is gaining attention for its effective design. The product helps prevent wear and tear on shoes, enhancing overall comfort and convenience for boaters. This development is set to revolutionize the way shoes are managed on boats, promising enhanced organization and cleanliness.

data.title: LexisNexis launches AI-driven IDVerse for Insurance to combat fraud, deepfakes. Helps US insurers protect customers.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, LexisNexis® Risk Solutions unveiled LexisNexis® IDVerse® for Insurance, an advanced AI-driven identity solution. This cutting-edge technology offers robust fraud and deepfake detection capabilities, ensuring enhanced security in the insurance sector. Despite its powerful features, LexisNexis® IDVerse® for Insurance prioritizes user experience by maintaining a low-friction process. This development marks a significant step towards safeguarding against fraud in the insurance industry, showcasing the company's commitment to innovation and security.

data.title: CPSC recalls Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kits due to risk of serious injury or death. View recalls at www.cpsc.gov.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) issued a recall for Sefudun 5% Minoxidil Hair Growth Serum Kits in cooperation with the involved companies. The recall was initiated due to the potential risk of serious injury or death to children. Consumers are urged to visit www.cpsc.gov for more information on the affected products.

data.title: Roche's fiscal 2025 sales: 61.5 billion Swiss Francs ($76.96 billion). Core operating profit up 13% to CHF 21.8 billion.
data.description: Roche Holdings AG reported sales of 61.5 billion Swiss Francs (approximately $76.96 billion) for fiscal 2025, representing a 7% increase (2% in CHF) fueled by high demand for pharmaceutical products and diagnostic solutions. The core operating profit also saw a significant boost, rising by 13% (5% in CHF) to CHF 21.8 billion, attributed to increased sales and improved efficiency. Additionally, core EPS surged by 11% (4% in CHF) to CHF 19.46 ($24.35). In the Pharmaceuticals Division, sales experienced a 9% climb (3% in CHF) to CHF 47.7 billion, driven by robust growth in medicines for severe ailments. The top five contributors to growth – Phesgo, Xolair, Ocrevus, Hemlibra, and Vabysmo – collectively generated sales of CHF 21.4 billion, marking a CHF 3.2 billion increase compared to 2024. Notably, Ocrevus sales spiked by 9% to CHF 7.01 billion, while Hemlibra sales saw an 11% rise.
